title: Kabieroun (species)
content:
{{Top|ga|leg}} {{Youmay|the alien species|their [[Kabieroun|homeworld]] or their [[Kabieroun system/Legends|home system]]}} {{Species |image=[[File:CompletelyRandomSciFiCreature-ROTM.png]] |name=Kabieroun |designation=[[Sentience/Legends|Sentient]]{{JournalCite|8|A Certain Point of View (short story)}} |class= |subspecies= |races= |height=Taller than most [[Human/Legends|Humans]] |length= |wingspan= |mass= |skincolor=[[Color/Legends|Green]][[Riders of the Maelstrom (adventure)|''Riders of the Maelstrom'']] |haircolor=White |feathers= |eyecolor=Black |distinctions=Curved neck, long snout, pointed [[Ear/Legends|ears]] |lifespan= |origin=[[Kabieroun]] |habitat= |diet= |language= }} {{Quote|The Empire is like that, little Crimson. From a distance, we may not feel danger—we're too far removed from its touch. But when it is upon us, we will hear and see only what the Empire desires.|Detien Kaileel|}} The '''Kabierouns''' were a [[Sentience/Legends|sentient]] [[Species/Legends|species]] indigenous to the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Kabieroun]] in the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories]]. Their physiology was roughly [[Humanoid/Legends|humanoid]], with a long, wrinkled snout that featured two dark [[Eye/Legends|eyes]] and a triangular [[Mouth/Legends|mouth]]. The Kabieroun face hung from a lengthy, muscular neck that swooped forward from a bulky torso. With a large frame, Kabierouns towered over most [[Human/Legends|Humans]]. At least some members of the species integrated into [[The galaxy/Legends|the greater galaxy]], attending schools and finding employment. However, after the rise of the [[New Order/Legends|New Order]], the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] [[Slavery/Legends|enslaved]] Kabierouns on their homeworld, and those members of the species abroad faced [[speciesism]]. [[Detien Kaileel]], a [[Color/Legends|green]]-skinned member of the species, served for [[Time/Legends|decades]] as a [[Law enforcement agency/Legends|security agent]] but turned to [[Smuggler/Legends|running]] [[Blaster/Legends|blasters]] to the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]] after the enslavement of his people. ==Appearance and biology== [[File:Kabieroun-AJ8.jpg|thumb|left|upright|Kabierouns had two dark eyes and a fleshy proboscis at the end of their long snout.]] Although the Kabieroun body was roughly [[Humanoid/Legends|humanoid]]—with two [[Limb/Legends|arms]], two legs, and a head—the frame was much bulkier and the torso much thicker than that of the typical [[Human/Legends|Human]]. This overlarge frame made the Kabierouns an imposing sight to smaller [[Species/Legends|species]], giving the [[Sentience/Legends|sentients]] an intimidating appearance that was bolstered by the great strength provided by rippling muscles visible in the chest, arms, and neck. While the upper arm was thin, the lower arm was bulky, with a bulge of flesh toward its underside. Scales covered the shoulders and arms, and the skin of at least some Kabierouns was green, its [[Color/Legends|color]] darkening while blushing. Each hand featured five long, suction-cup-tipped fingers—two of them thumbs—and each foot featured at least two digits. A bulge grew from the small of the back, and a thin layer of white hair covered the Kabieroun body. Kabierouns had two [[Gender/Legends|sexes]], male and female. The Kabieroun neck was long and arched, swooping up, forward, then down before jutting forward horizontally from its midpoint. Although the back of the neck was rounded, the underside was flat until the the midpoint, when it was rounded and wrinkled. The enormous head was characterized by a cleft skull that extended downward as a cylinder; this was flanked by two large, flat, pointed [[Ear/Legends|ears]]. The Kabieroun face hung downward from the lowest point of the head, surrounded by waves of wrinkles. The visage itself consisted of two large, black [[Eye/Legends|eyes]], a thin, fingerlike proboscis with two nostrils, and a triangular [[Mouth/Legends|mouth]] with a red tongue, which remained hidden behind the rest of the snout when seen from the front. Kabieroun lips were pliable enough to allow the species to smile and make other expressions. ==Society and culture== Kabierouns could manipulate [[The galaxy/Legends|galactic]]-standard technology and even sit in chairs designed for beings with different physiologies. They could speak [[Galactic Basic Standard/Legends|Basic]], although some members spoke that [[Language/Legends|language]] with a strong accent.> One Kabieroun who had integrated into the [[galactic community]], [[Detien Kaileel]], wore a form-fitting, sleeveless jumpsuit and sported an [[Piercing/Legends|earring]] in his left ear. Kabierouns expressed emotions in ways similar to Humans; Detien Kaileel, for instance, pulled on his earring when embarrassed, smiled when content, shook his head when incredulous, and trembled when nervous. Kaileel also expressed emotions of annoyance, gentleness, suspicion, and discontent. ==History== The Kabieroun [[Homeworld/Legends|homeworld]] was the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Kabieroun]]. It was located in the [[Nijune sector/Legends|Nijune sector]],{{SW|url=news/star-wars-the-essential-atlas-online-companion|text=''Star Wars: The Essential Atlas'' Online Companion|int=Star Wars: The Essential Atlas Online Companion}} part of the [[New Territories/Legends|New Territories]]''[[The Essential Atlas]]'' of the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim]]. After the rise of the [[New Order/Legends|New Order]], the Kabieroun homeworld was seized by the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]], and much of the planet's populace was [[Slavery/Legends|enslaved]]. [[Standard year/Legends|Years]] later, during the [[Thrawn campaign]], the planet fell within neutral territory, neither part of the [[Imperial Remnant]] nor the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]]. From [[10 ABY/Legends|10]] to [[11 ABY/Legends|11 ABY]], the Kabierouns' home planet fell within Imperial Remnant territory. ==Kabierouns in the galaxy== {{Quote|You don't support the Rebel Alliance, do you?''"''
''"''Let's just say I disagree with the Empire's method of resolving this conflict.|Celia Durasha and Detien Kaileel|}} Those Kabierouns who left their homeworld for the greater galaxy during the reign of [[Galactic Emperor/Legends|Emperor]] [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine]] faced [[Speciesism|discrimination]]. Detien Kaileel was among those members of his species to emigrate before the institution of the Empire and before the enslavement of his people. He entered the [[Balaidas Academy]] to study [[Law enforcement agency/Legends|police]] science, one of the last [[Alien/Legends|non-Humans]] to do so before the institution closed admissions to non-Humans under the rule of the New Order. After graduating with honors, Kaileel served as a member of the [[Mid-Rim Sentinel Agency]] for over 20 years, leaving the organization after being wounded while pursuing a [[Crime lord/Legends|crime lord]]. The Kabieroun then took what he considered to be a safer position as head of security on the [[Luxury liner/Legends|luxury liner]] [[Kuari Princess/Legends|''Kuari Princess'']]. Kaileel grew increasingly concerned about what he perceived to be the misdeeds of the Galactic Empire. The security officer eventually began to use his security clearance to [[Smuggler/Legends|smuggle]] [[Blaster/Legends|guns]] to [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]] operatives on the planet [[Mantooine/Legends|Mantooine]], part of the ''Kuari Princess''{{'s}} route. At some point in the years leading up to the [[Battle of Endor/Legends|Battle of Endor]], his efforts came to the attention of the [[Imperial Security Bureau/Legends|Imperial Security Bureau]], and the Human agent [[Adion Lang]] was dispatched to the ''Princess'' to arrest the Kabieroun.{{CSWECite|II|93|Kaileel, Detien}} Despite the intervention of Kaileel's friends, [[Celia Durasha]] and [[Dap Nechel]], a firefight broke out between Kaileel and Lang, and the Kabieroun was shot and [[Death/Legends|killed]]. ==Behind the scenes== [[File:RotM.jpg|thumb|upright|right|''Riders of the Maelstrom''{{'s}} cover inspired Charlene Newcomb's look at the Kabierouns.]] {{Quote|I told Charlene to write a story involving this scene so I could feature the color artwork in the Journal. She went to work and submitted "A Certain Point of View," in the plot of which she managed to highlight several elements of the painting.|Peter M. Schweighofer|''[[Tales from the Empire]]'', "Introduction: A Galaxy Filled with Stories"}} The cover of [[Riders of the Maelstrom (adventure)|''Riders of the Maelstrom'']], an adventure for ''[[Star Wars: The Roleplaying Game]]'' published by [[West End Games]] in [[1989]], depicts a large, green-skinned alien seated at a [[Hologame table/Legends|hologame table]] with a Human woman as a smaller alien looks on. Although this image, painted by [[Daniel Horne]], appears on the book's cover, the adventure itself contains no information about the game players. Years later, [[Peter M. Schweighofer]], editor of the ''[[Star Wars Adventure Journal]]'', wanted to use the image in his publication. He challenged author [[Charlene Newcomb]] to write a short story based on the painting, and the result was "[[A Certain Point of View (short story)|A Certain Point of View]]," published in [[Star Wars Adventure Journal 8|''Star Wars Adventure Journal'' 8]] in [[November]] [[1995]]. Newcomb identified the green alien—Detien Kaileel—as well as his companions in the image. She also named his species as the Kabierouns and provided some background on the species' enslavement by the Empire. The game statistics used for Kaileel indicate that the Kabierouns enjoy no game bonuses over Humans and other species. ==Appearances== {{Mediacat|imagecat=Images of Kabierouns}} *{{JournalCite|8|A Certain Point of View (short story)|reprint=1}} {{1st}} {{1stID|Kabieroun}} *[[Riders of the Maelstrom (adventure)|''Riders of the Maelstrom'']] {{Reprint|Riders of the Maelstrom (adventure)}} {{1stp}} {{Co}} ==Sources== *{{CSWECite|II|93|Kaileel, Detien}} *''[[The Essential Reader's Companion]]'' ==Notes and references== {{Reflist}} [[Category:Outer Rim Territories sentient species]] {{RelatedCategories |[[Category:Kabierouns]] }}
